change.purpose: Bind the canonical Cloudflare environment requirements document and validator to a path-scoped, exact-checkout CI contract without reading secret values.

verified: GitHub accepted the workflow update on the default branch
the workflow now watches the canonical requirements document, validator, tests, deployment workflow, and workflow-contract validator
the workflow proves an exact clean checkout before running both contract suites
the workflow uses contents:read only and does not reference Cloudflare secret values

runtime_status.contract_job: not yet observed through the available connector
runtime_status.combined_status_observation: only unrelated Vercel build-rate-limit failures were returned for the commit

epistemic_limit: A passing configuration-contract workflow proves only that committed declarations and workflow references satisfy the validators. It does not prove that GitHub environment secrets exist, that a token is valid, that Cloudflare authorizes it, or that a Pages deployment resolved.
